statement: The course will cover topics from Galileo and Newton to relativity and quantum mechanics.
The target audience for the course is limited to physics majors only.
The lectures will be taped as part of an experimental pilot program funded by the Hewlett Foundation.
The course will focus on the study of living objects and their properties.
Kinematics is a branch of physics that deals with the reason behind why an object moves or changes its motion.
The course will start with complex examples and gradually simplify them.
A mathematical point is a non-living, spatially extended object.
The unit of time in the course will be measured in hours.
Kinematics only deals with objects that have no spatial extent and move along a line.
Units are not necessary when solving problems in kinematics.
